text.title: structured-tool-errors Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for shaping tool errors so an agent loop can branch deterministically.

rule.statement: Every tool error response MUST be a JSON object `{error: {code, message, recoveryHint, traceId, ...}}` with `code` (UPPER_SNAKE machine code), `message` (one human sentence, no stack trace), `recoveryHint` (closed enum), `traceId` (correlation ID). Optional: `retry_after_seconds`, sanitised `details`.
rule.rationale: The closed-shape envelope is what makes downstream policy mapping deterministic. Studies measured 30%+ drop in dead-end retry loops vs free-form messages.
rule.reference: JSON Schema for the envelope; copy into tool / MCP contract.

rule.statement: `traceId` is required on every error response, ≥8 chars, ideally a ULID or UUID. Never omit; never use the message text as a stand-in. The traceId is what humans search for in logs when the agent escalates.
rule.rationale: Without traceId, post-mortem on an escalated failure requires reading every log line; with it, the human grep is one query. The cost of generating a ULID is negligible.

rule.statement: `recoveryHint` MUST be exactly one of `RETRY_LATER | CHECK_INPUT | TRY_ALTERNATIVE | REPORT_TO_USER | NEEDS_AUTH`. Any other value is a contract violation. Never invent new hints inline — extend the enum repo-wide if a new category is genuinely needed.
rule.rationale: Free-form hint prose forces the agent (and the runner) to parse natural language to decide the next action. A closed enum compiles to a switch statement.
rule.reference: One-line semantic per enum value; ship as a tool-author quick reference.

rule.statement: The agent runner MUST map each `recoveryHint` to exactly one action: `RETRY_LATER` → wait `retry_after_seconds` clamped 1-300s and retry up to a per-tool retry budget; `CHECK_INPUT` → return to the model with no automatic retry; `TRY_ALTERNATIVE` → return to model, signal to pick a different tool; `REPORT_TO_USER` → halt loop, surface to user; `NEEDS_AUTH` → halt loop, request credentials.
rule.rationale: Without explicit dispatch, the model invents its own retry policy each turn, often retrying `INVALID_ARGUMENT` (waste) or escalating `RATE_LIMITED` (premature). Explicit dispatch is cheaper and safer.
rule.reference: YAML map from hint → action with default retry budgets.

rule.statement: `message` MUST NOT contain stack frames, file paths, line numbers, or upstream JSON. `details` MUST NOT contain raw stack traces. Replace with semantic codes and sanitised fields (e.g., `"upstream_status": 503`, not `"raw_response": ""`).

rationale.source: OWASP API Security Top 10, A09 — Information Exposure

rule.rationale: Stack traces leak implementation, fill context budget, and confuse the model into pretend-debugging. Sanitisation is a security AND correctness gain.
